hypothesis: If a computational framework combining fragment-based polymer generation, DFT-refined force field parameters, multi-stage MD/GCMC simulations, and Bayesian optimization with pre-trained molecular representations is applied to the chemical space of polyamines, then it will efficiently identify high-performance solid-state CO2 capture materials with superior adsorption capacities compared to known reference polyamines within a limited simulation budget, because the surrogate-guided search can exploit structural–property relationships encoded in pre-trained embeddings to prioritize promising candidates without exhaustively evaluating the full hypothetical polymer library.
